title: Bridge Financing 101: When Speed Beats Rate

blocks.text: When paying more for speed is the right call — and how to structure a bridge so the exit is already in place. Bridge debt is the most misunderstood tool in commercial financing: expensive when it's the wrong answer, cheap when it's the right one.

items: A closing deadline the conventional process can't meet
A property that won't qualify for term debt until it's stabilized
Equity trapped in one asset that's needed to close another
A discounted purchase where certainty wins the deal

blocks.text: A bridge without a defined exit is just expensive debt. Every bridge we arrange is structured backwards from its takeout — the term financing, sale, or refinance that repays it — with the timeline, conditions, and lender for that exit identified before the bridge funds.
Do the math on the whole trade, not the rate. Three points of bridge cost that captures a below-market acquisition is the cheapest money you'll ever borrow.
